segments.text: Truly my soul waits silently for God; from Him comes my salvation.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He alone is my rock and my salvation; He is my defense; I shall not be greatly moved.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: How long will you attack a man? You shall be slain, all of you, like a leaning wall and a tottering fence.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: They only consult to cast him down from his high position; they delight in lies; they bless with their mouth, but they curse inwardly.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: inwardly: Heb. in their inward parts

segments.text: My soul, wait silently for God alone, for my expectation is from Him.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He only is my rock and my salvation; He is my defense; I shall not be moved.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: In God is my salvation and my glory; the rock of my strength, and my refuge, is in God.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Trust in Him at all times, you people; pour out your heart before Him; God is a refuge for us.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Surely men of low degree are a vapor, men of high degree are a lie; if they are weighed on the scales, they are altogether lighter than vapor.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Do not trust in oppression, nor vainly hope in robbery; if riches increase, do not set your heart on them.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: God has spoken once, twice I have heard this: that power belongs to God.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Also to You, O Lord, belongs mercy; for You render to each one according to his work.
segments.speaker: God
